tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria with low urine specific gravity, indicating possible nephrotic syndrome or advanced kidney disease. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Mildly elevated urine ketones are present. HbA1c is low, possibly due to anemia or recent changes in glucose metabolism. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and underlying causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
